title: Generated file detection via content markers
description:
## Background
srcwalk detects generated files via content markers (`@generated`, `DO NOT EDIT`) using memchr SIMD and filename patterns. Scry lacks this. Generated files (lock files, minified bundles) should not be read in full as they waste tokens.

## Scope
Add generated file detection module in fff-symbol and wire it into read command.

## Implementation
1. Create `crates/fff-symbol/src/detection.rs`:
   ```rust
   /// Check if file content contains generated-file markers using memchr SIMD.
   pub fn is_generated_by_content(content: &[u8]) -> bool;
   /// Check if filename suggests a generated/lock file.
   pub fn is_generated_by_name(path: &Path) -> bool;
   ```
   `is_generated_by_content`: scan first 4096 bytes for:
   - `@generated`, `DO NOT EDIT`, `auto-generated`, `Code generated by`, `@codegenerated`
   Use `memchr::memmem::find` (SIMD-accelerated). Add `memchr` dep to fff-symbol Cargo.toml if needed.
   
   `is_generated_by_name`: match lock files (package-lock.json, Cargo.lock, go.sum, etc.) and *.min.js, *.pb.go, *.generated.*

2. Register in `crates/fff-symbol/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod detection;`

3. Wire into `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/read.rs`:
   - After resolving path, check if generated
   - If generated, auto-switch to outline/signatures with a `[generated file]` note

4. Wire into `crates/fff-mcp/src/server.rs`:
   - `scry_read` tool: add `[generated file]` note when applicable

## Files Created
- `crates/fff-symbol/src/detection.rs` (~60 lines)

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-symbol/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/read.rs`
- `crates/fff-mcp/src/server.rs` (optional)

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~80 lines

title: detailed callees with call-site args and assignments
description:
## Background
srcwalk's --detailed callees show ordered call sites with arguments, assignment context, and return variable tracking. Scry's callees only show flat `name @ path:line`. This is the most valuable gap fix for understanding how a symbol is actually used.

## Scope
Add --detailed flag to callees that shows call sites with args, assignment context, and return tracking.

## Implementation
1. Create `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees_detail.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub struct CallSite {
       pub line: u32,
       pub callee: String,
       pub call_text: String,
       pub call_prefix: Option<String>,
       pub args: Vec<String>,
       pub return_var: Option<String>,
       pub is_return: bool,
   }
   
   pub fn extract_call_sites(content: &str, lang: Lang, start_line: u32, end_line: u32, known_symbols: &HashSet<String>) -> Vec<CallSite>;
   ```
   ~150 lines. Manual AST walk (no streaming_iterator dependency).
   Per call expression: extract callee name, arguments, call_prefix, check for return statement, check assignment context (let x = foo()), dedup by (line, call_text).

2. Create `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees_format.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub fn format_call_site(site: &CallSite) -> String;
   fn format_call_with_args(site: &CallSite) -> String;
   fn compact_arg(arg: &str) -> String;
   ```
   ~60 lines. Output: `L42 result = foo(arg1=x, arg2=y)` or `L88 bar()` or `L91 ->ret baz(ctx)`

3. Modify `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees.rs`:
   - Add --detailed flag, --filter option
   - When detailed: use extract_call_sites instead of collect_callees
   - CalleesOutput gains sites field

4. Wire into flow command for richer callee display.
5. MCP: add detailed param to scry_callees tool.

## Files Created
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees_detail.rs` (~150 lines)
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees_format.rs` (~60 lines)

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/mod.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/flow.rs`
- `crates/fff-mcp/src/server.rs`

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~240 lines
close_reason: implemented detailed callees with call-site args and assignments

title: skip-hubs explicit user override for callers BFS
description:
## Background
srcwalk lets users explicitly skip hub symbols during BFS callers with `--skip-hubs "println,eprintln,fmt"`. Scry only has auto-hub promotion (hub_guard threshold) but no user override.

## Scope
Add --skip-hubs CSV flag that lets users explicitly name symbols to exclude from BFS frontier (beyond auto-promotion).

## Implementation
1. Add flag to `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callers.rs`:
   ```rust
   #[arg(long, default_value = "")]
   pub skip_hubs: String,
   ```

2. In `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callers_bfs.rs`:
   - Parse skip_hubs CSV into HashSet<String> at start of run_bfs()
   - Pass into BfsConfig struct
   - In frontier loop, before processing each name:
     ```rust
     if user_hubs.contains(name) && depth > 1 {
         stats.hubs_skipped.push(name.clone());
         continue;
     }
     ```
   - Root symbol (depth 1) always explored even if in skip list (srcwalk behavior)
   - Add hubs_skipped: Vec<String> to BfsTelemetry

3. Output: add skipped hubs to telemetry section of text output

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callers.rs` -- add flag (~3 lines)
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callers_bfs.rs` -- parse CSV + filter (~25 lines)

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~28 lines

title: Suspicious-hop directory-proximity detection
Multi-scope find with deduplication
description:
## Background
srcwalk supports multiple --scope directories with deduplication, overlap detection, and per-scope result headers. Scry only accepts a single root.

## Scope
Add --scope multi-value flag to CLI and merge results across multiple directories.

## Implementation
1. Add to `crates/fff-cli/src/cli.rs`:
   ```rust
   #[arg(long, global = true, num_args = 1..)]
   pub scope: Vec<PathBuf>,
   ```
   Keep root as primary default. scope overrides when provided.

2. In find/dispatch commands, when multiple scopes provided:
   - Run Engine per-scope
   - Merge results with HashSet<String> dedup key "path:line"
   - Render with per-scope headers in text output

3. When single scope, behavior unchanged.

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-cli/src/cli.rs` -- add flag
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/find.rs` -- multi-scope runner
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/dispatch.rs` -- multi-scope dispatch

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~100 lines

title: Shell completions via clap_complete
description:
## Background
srcwalk generates shell completions via clap_complete. Scry currently has no completion support. This is the smallest, most self-contained gap fix.

## Scope
Add \`--completions <shell>\` flag to the scry CLI that generates completion scripts for bash/zsh/fish/powershell/elvish.

## Implementation
1. Add \`clap_complete\` dependency to \`crates/fff-cli/Cargo.toml\`
2. Add to \`crates/fff-cli/src/cli.rs\`:
   \`\`\`rust
   #[arg(long, value_enum, global = true)]
   pub completions: Option<clap_complete::Shell>,
   \`\`\`
3. In \`crates/fff-cli/src/main.rs\`, before command dispatch:
   \`\`\`rust
   if let Some(shell) = cli.completions {
       clap_complete::generate(shell, &mut Cli::command(), "scry", &mut std::io::stdout());
       return Ok(());
   }
   \`\`\`

## Files Modified
- \`crates/fff-cli/Cargo.toml\` — add clap_complete dep
- \`crates/fff-cli/src/cli.rs\` — add completions flag
- \`crates/fff-cli/src/main.rs\` — add generation logic

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~9 lines new code

title: JS/TS artifact mode with export anchor extraction
description:
## Background
srcwalk has artifact mode: support for reading compiled/bundled JS/TS files with export anchor extraction, byte-range section reads, and IIFE detection. Scry lacks this entirely. For JS/TS projects with bundled/minified output, this is critical for code intelligence.

## Scope
Port srcwalk's artifact.rs into fff-symbol and wire into read, callees, flow, deps commands.

## Implementation
1. Create `crates/fff-symbol/src/artifact.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub struct ArtifactAnchor {
       pub line: u32,
       pub kind: &'static str, // "export" | "mod"
       pub name: String,
   }
   
   pub fn is_artifact_js_ts_file(path: &Path) -> bool;
   pub fn extract_artifact_anchors(content: &str) -> (Vec<ArtifactAnchor>, usize);
   pub fn search_anchor_matches(content: &str, query: &str) -> Vec<ArtifactAnchor>;
   pub fn read_js_ts_symbol_section(path: &Path, symbol: &str, budget: Option<u64>) -> Option<Result<String>>;
   ```
   Internal helpers (~200 lines of porting from srcwalk's artifact.rs):
   - extract_es_export_names: ES `export { ... }`, `export default/function/class/const/let/var`
   - extract_named_commonjs_exports: `module.exports.X`, `exports.X`
   - extract_umd_global_exports: UMD wrapper patterns
   - extract_named_amd_modules: `define("name", ...)`
   - clean_export_name: identifier sanitizer
   - find_js_ts_symbol_span: tree-sitter AST symbol location in JS/TS
   - render_artifact_symbol_section: formatted output with byte span metadata
   - render_artifact_byte_section: raw byte-range section
   - compact_artifact_text: truncate with ... marker

2. Register in `crates/fff-symbol/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod artifact;`

3. Wire into read command: add --artifact flag, inject anchor block, support bytes:start-end syntax
4. Wire into callees/flow: add --artifact flag for artifact-mode callee resolution
5. Wire into deps: use anchors as pseudo-imports for JS/TS files
6. MCP: add artifact param to scry_read, scry_callees, scry_flow

## Files Created
- `crates/fff-symbol/src/artifact.rs` (~200 lines)
- `crates/fff-symbol/src/artifact_test.rs` (unit tests, ~80 lines)

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-symbol/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/read.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/flow.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/deps.rs`
- `crates/fff-mcp/src/server.rs`

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~450 lines
close_reason: implemented artifact module with export anchor extraction in fff-symbol

title: AhoCorasick batch symbol search
description:
## Background
srcwalk uses AhoCorasick for multi-symbol queries ("A,B,C") in a single file walk pass. Scry does N separate lookups. This is a significant performance win for batch symbol queries.

## Scope
Add AhoCorasick-based batch symbol search to fff-symbol and wire into dispatch.

## Implementation
1. Check if aho-corasick is already in `crates/fff-symbol/Cargo.toml`. If not, add it.

2. Create `crates/fff-symbol/src/batch.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub struct BatchSymbolSearch {
       patterns: Vec<String>,
       ac: aho_corasick::AhoCorasick,
   }
   
   pub struct BatchResult {
       pub symbol: String,
       pub locations: Vec<SymbolLocation>,
   }
   
   impl BatchSymbolSearch {
       pub fn new(symbols: &[Vec<String>]) -> Self;
       pub fn search(&self, index: &SymbolIndex) -> Vec<BatchResult>;
   }
   ```
   Logic:
   1. Build AhoCorasick from symbol names.
   2. For each code file in symbol index, read bytes.
   3. ac.find_overlapping(bytes) for candidate matches.
   4. Confirm each candidate against symbol_index.
   5. Bucket results per-symbol.
   
   ~100 lines.

3. Register in `crates/fff-symbol/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod batch;`

4. Wire into `crates/fff-engine/src/dispatch.rs`:
   - When query is classified as multi-symbol (comma-separated), route to BatchSymbolSearch
   - Fallback to single-query path when only one symbol

5. Wire into `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/dispatch.rs`:
   - Parse comma-separated queries and route to batch mode

## Files Created
- `crates/fff-symbol/src/batch.rs` (~100 lines)

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-symbol/Cargo.toml` (maybe add aho-corasick)
- `crates/fff-symbol/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fff-engine/src/dispatch.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/dispatch.rs`

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~130 lines

title: Section disambiguation for bare filenames
description:
## Background
When a bare filename matches multiple files, srcwalk uses heuristics to pick the "primary" one: prefer non-test, non-vendor files closest to root. Scry's `maybe_resolve_bare()` returns Ambiguous with sorted candidates but never auto-picks.

## Scope
Add a primary-pick heuristic to the read command's bare filename resolution.

## Implementation
1. Add `pick_primary()` to `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/read.rs`:
   ```rust
   fn pick_primary(candidates: &[PathBuf], root: &Path) -> Option<usize> {
       const NON_PROD: &[&str] = &["test","tests","__tests__","spec","specs",
           "vendor","node_modules","dist","build","target",".git"];
       // Score: +100 if no NON_PROD segment, +depth bonus (shorter = higher),
       // +50 if in src/ dir. If clear winner (gap > 150), return it.
   }
   ```
   ~40 lines.

2. In `maybe_resolve_bare()`: when Ambiguous, try `pick_primary()`. If a clear winner, return Found instead.

3. If no clear winner, keep current Ambiguous behavior with candidate list.

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/read.rs` -- new function + wire (~45 lines)

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~45 lines

title: Session dedup tracker for BFS expansion
description:
## Background
srcwalk has a per-invocation Session tracker that records which "path:line" locations have been expanded, preventing the same source body from being re-expanded during BFS cascade fallbacks. Scry lacks this — callers_bfs and callees_bfs can duplicate expansion work.

## Scope
Create a lightweight session dedup tracker and wire it into callers_bfs, callees_bfs, and flow commands.

## Implementation
1. Create \`crates/fff-cli/src/commands/session.rs\`:
   \`\`\`rust
   pub struct Session {
       expanded: std::cell::RefCell<HashSet<String>>, // "path:line" -> already inlined
   }
   impl Session {
       pub fn new() -> Self;
       pub fn is_expanded(&self, path: &Path, line: u32) -> bool;
       pub fn record_expand(&self, path: &Path, line: u32);
   }
   \`\`\`
   Uses RefCell (not Mutex) since each CLI invocation is single-threaded.

2. Wire into \`crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callers_bfs.rs\`:
   - Create Session at top of run_bfs()
   - Before expanding a caller hit's source context, check session.is_expanded()
   - After expanding, call session.record_expand()

3. Wire into \`crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees_bfs.rs\` — same pattern.

4. Wire into \`crates/fff-cli/src/commands/flow.rs\` — pass session through to callees/callers so dedup works across the combined flow.

## Reference
Ported from srcwalk's \`src/session.rs\` (37 lines). Nearly identical but uses RefCell instead of Mutex.

## Files Created
- \`crates/fff-cli/src/commands/session.rs\` — new (~30 lines)

## Files Modified
- \`crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callers_bfs.rs\`
- \`crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callees_bfs.rs\`
- \`crates/fff-cli/src/commands/flow.rs\`

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~65 lines total

title: Enhanced import resolution for deps command
description:
## Background
Scry already has deps_resolve.rs with extract_imports() and resolve_import() for 16 languages. Compared to srcwalk's imports.rs, it is missing: CommonJS require() extraction, @/~/ path alias resolution for JS/TS, Elixir import handling, and some edge cases in Rust/JS/Python resolution.

## Scope
Enhance deps_resolve.rs to match srcwalk's coverage while keeping the superior AST-based approach.

## Implementation
1. Read current `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/deps_resolve.rs` to identify exact gaps.

2. Add CommonJS require() extraction for JS/TS:
   - In node_to_import, add case for call nodes that are require("...")
   - Extract string argument from require() call

3. Add @/ and ~/ path alias resolution for JS/TS:
   - @/ -> try root/src/
   - ~/ -> try root/
   - Add to resolve_import() after relative-path check

4. Add Elixir import source extraction:
   - alias, import, use, require detection
   - Module-to-file mapping: MyApp.Module -> my_app/module.ex

5. Add tests covering new resolvers.

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/deps_resolve.rs` (~120 lines of additions)
- Unit tests (~60 lines)

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~180 lines

title: View mode cascade for file reads
description:
## Background
srcwalk cascades read modes when budget exceeded: full -> outline -> signatures. Scry just truncates. This provides better information density for AI agents: instead of a truncated mid-line file, they get a complete structural view.

## Scope
Add view mode cascade that degrades gracefully from full to outline to signatures when budget exceeded.

## Implementation
1. Create `crates/fff-budget/src/cascade.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub enum ViewMode { Full, Outline, Signatures }
   
   pub struct CascadeResult {
       pub mode: ViewMode,
       pub body: String,
       pub kept_bytes: usize,
       pub footer_bytes: usize,
   }
   
   /// Try full; if budget exceeded, fall back to outline; if still over, signatures.
   pub fn cascade_read(
       content: &str,
       outline: &[OutlineEntry],
       budget: BudgetSplit,
       filter_level: FilterLevel,
   ) -> CascadeResult;
   ```
   Logic:
   1. Apply filter_level to content.
   2. If fits in body budget, return Full.
   3. Render outline from OutlineEntry list. If fits, return Outline.
   4. Extract signatures only (name + signature field, strip children/bodies). If fits, return Signatures.
   5. Last resort: smart_truncate the signatures.
   
   ~120 lines.

2. Add signature extraction helper (~30 lines) that walks OutlineEntry and produces signature-only view.

3. Register in `crates/fff-budget/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod cascade;`

4. Modify `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/read.rs`:
   - Replace truncation path in run() with cascade_read()
   - Add --signatures mode as explicit lowest-fidelity option
   - ViewMode flows into ReadOutput struct

5. Modify `crates/fff-engine/src/dispatch.rs`:
   - Engine::read() returns ViewMode in response

6. MCP `scry_read` tool: include view_mode in response

## Key Design Decision
Cascade should NOT re-parse files. Uses pre-computed &[OutlineEntry] from OutlineCache. No redundant tree-sitter work.

## Files Created
- `crates/fff-budget/src/cascade.rs` (~120 lines)

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-budget/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/read.rs`
- `crates/fff-engine/src/dispatch.rs`
- `crates/fff-mcp/src/server.rs` (optional)

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~220 lines
## Blocked by: generated file detection (reads the same detection module, can be done in parallel though)
close_reason: cascade module created in fff-budget with tests, ready for wiring

title: count-by aggregation for callers
description:
## Background
srcwalk has `--count-by` that aggregates callers by a field (file, symbol, package). Scry shows individual hits only. This is useful for getting a high-level view of where a symbol is called from without listing every single call site.

## Scope
Add --count-by flag to callers command with File/Symbol/Package aggregation modes.

## Implementation
1. Add to `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callers.rs`:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, ValueEnum)]
   pub enum CountByField {
       File,
       Symbol,
       Package,
   }
   
   #[arg(long, value_enum)]
   pub count_by: Option<CountByField>,
   ```

2. After collecting hits, aggregate:
   - `File`: group by `hit.path`, show `path (N callers)`
   - `Symbol`: group by `hit.enclosing`, show `symbol (N call sites)`
   - `Package`: group by `package_root(hit.path, root)`, show `pkg (N callers)`
   Use HashMap grouping + sort by count descending.

3. When --count-by is set, output aggregated table instead of individual hits. JSON mode emits grouped arrays.

## Files Modified
- `crates/fff-cli/src/commands/callers.rs` -- flag + enum + aggregation logic + render (~60 lines)

## Constraint: No changes to crates/fff-core/

## Estimated: ~60 lines
